## Structure

TCP HandshakeA — server's initial handshake greeting on the
TCP gameserver connection. Fixed 3-byte body `80 01 66`.

```
[0..1]   80 01                  TCP opcode (constant)
[2]      0x66                   handshake-A constant byte (= 'f' ASCII)
```

All 71 retail observations are byte-identical: `80 01 66`. NO
variation.

## Variants

Single 3-byte form. Pure constant.

## Observed contexts

Server-to-client TCP handshake step A — the first thing the
server emits on a freshly-accepted TCP connection. The client
expects this as a "server is alive" signal before continuing
the auth flow.

Same 3-byte format used in:
- gameserver TCP HandshakeA (this packet)
- patchserver TCP PatchHandshakeA (different listener but same
  handshake-A wire shape)
- infoserver TCP HandshakeA

## Open questions

- Why specifically `0x66`? Possibly a hardcoded NC2 protocol
  version magic. Constant across all 71 retail observations and
  all 3 server flavors.

## Server-side handler

`server.gameserver.packets.server_tcp.HandshakeA` — emits the
verified 3-byte body. Fired immediately on TCP-connection
accept.

Companion packets:
- `server.patchserver.packets.server_tcp.PatchHandshakeA`
- `server.infoserver.packets.server_tcp.HandshakeA`

All three are 3-byte `80 01 66` constant — different listeners,
same handshake-A signal.
